description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit, featuring a rectangular solar panel mounted atop a low-profile base structure. The solar panel is dark, likely composed of photovoltaic cells, and is set at a noticeable tilt to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and possibly reinforced plastic, with visible mounting brackets and support elements. There are several protrusions and small components on the base, which may include electrical housings or connection points. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and efficient energy collection. The unit is suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, small utility areas, or dedicated solar installations.
description: A compact, tilted solar panel mounted on a sturdy rectangular base with visible support and electrical components. Designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the tilted solar panel and the main base structure, with some mounting details visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angle of the solar panel and the depth of the base, with support brackets more apparent.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the panel and additional base features, including possible electrical housings.
description_view_3: Angled view provides a perspective on both the panel tilt and the arrangement of components on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum) frame and support brackets
